title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Education
Institut d’Études Politiques de Paris (Sciences Po) · Paris, France Master of Research in Economics · August 2023 – June 2025 GPA: 4.0/4.0 (Converted by Institution)
Bard College · Annandale-on-Hudson, United States Bachelor of Arts in Economics · August 2019 – May 2023 GPA: 3.8/4.0 Awards: Levy Economics Institute Prize (2023); OSUN Certificate in Public Policy and Economic Analysis (2023); Amicus Foundation Scholarship (2021)

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Skills & Coursework
Technical Skills: R; STATA; Python; Julia; LaTeX; Salesforce; Microsoft Office; Optical Character Recognition; Census Matching; Panel and Cross-sectional Econometrics; DSGE Modeling; Randomized Control Trials
Languages: English (Native); French (B1); Khmer/Cambodian (Basic Work Proficiency)
Graduate Coursework: Advanced Macroeconomics I, II, III; Advanced Microeconomics I, II; Advanced Econometrics I, II, III; Development Economics; Public Economics; Economic History; Computational Economics; Political Economy; International Economics

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Professional Experience
Penn Wharton Budget Model · Philadelphia, United States Analyst (Full-Time) · June 2026 – Present
Department of Economics, Trinity College Dublin · Dublin, Ireland Predoctoral Research Assistant (Full-Time) · September 2025 – June 2026

Consulted on data processing and econometric methodology for research papers
Developed OCR (Python), census matching and cleaning (STATA) models to generate a large database of Russian Empire administrative and business surveys
Built webscraping models (Python) to construct databases of 100,000 foreigners in pre-revolution Russia and to extract tables and graphs from House Commons Papers
Developed VLM- and LLM-based OCR pipelines to convert historical PDFs into usable data
Tested and improved OCR models on the 1845–1914 Thom’s Street Directory reports from an HPC

The Economic Journal, Royal Economic Society · Paris, France Replicator (Part-Time) · August 2024 – June 2025

Replicated journal paper submissions under the supervision of Dr. Florian Oswald
Identified errors in submission code and provided recommendations

Kiel Institute for the World Economy · Remote Research Intern (Full-Time) · July 2024 – September 2024

Supported research by Dr. Christoph Trebesch and Dr. Johannes Marzian on militarization, public finance, and geopolitics; contributed to a working paper
Collected, cleaned, and analyzed historical public finance data for 20+ advanced economies (1870–2020) in R and STATA
Produced 200+ visualizations for exploratory analysis

Ceres, Inc. · Remote Financial Regulation Intern (Full-Time) · June 2023 – August 2023

Produced reports on international, federal, and state financial regulation policies
Wrote an 11-page internal report on improving the Federal Reserve’s climate-risk scenario analysis

Bard College Department of Economics · Annandale, United States Economics Tutor (Part-Time) · March 2022 – May 2023
Mekong Strategic Group · Phnom Penh, Cambodia Analytics Intern (Full-Time) · June 2022 – August 2022
OSUN – Economic Democracy Initiative · Annandale, United States Student Fellow (Full-Time) · June 2021 – August 2021
Office of Illinois State Senator Heather Steans · Chicago, United States Summer Intern (Full-Time) · June 2020 – August 2020

title: Research & Experience
text:
Analyst · June 2026 – Present
Fiscal and economic policy analysis at one of the nation’s leading nonpartisan budget modeling institutions.




Predoctoral Research Assistant · September 2025 – June 2026

Consulted on data processing and econometric methodology for research papers
Developed OCR (Python), census matching and cleaning (STATA) models to extract data from and generate a large database of Russian Empire administrative and business surveys
Built a webscraping model (Python) to collect and construct a database of economic and social characteristics of 100,000 foreigners in pre-revolution Russia
Built a webscraping model (Python) to locate and collect tables and graphs from House Commons Papers and Command Papers, then developed a VLM- and LLM-based OCR pipeline to convert these PDFs into usable data
Tested, ran, and improved OCR models (Python) on the 1845–1914 Thom’s Street Directory reports remotely from an HPC using a combination of VLMs and LLMs to produce usable digitized economic data





Replicator · August 2024 – June 2025

Replicated journal paper submissions to The Economic Journal under the supervision of Dr. Florian Oswald
Identified errors in submission code and provided recommendations to fix them





Research Intern · July 2024 – September 2024

Supported the research of Dr. Christoph Trebesch and Dr. Johannes Marzian on militarization, social welfare, public finance, and geopolitics, contributing to the release of a working paper
Collected, cleaned, and analyzed large datasets on macroeconomic historical public finance for over 20 advanced economies spanning 1870–2020 in R and STATA
Developed empirical identification methodology and produced over 200 visualizations for exploratory analysis





Financial Regulation Intern · June 2023 – August 2023

Produced reports on new international, federal, and state financial regulation policies
Wrote an 11-page internal report on improving the Federal Reserve’s climate-risk scenario analysis, supporting Ceres’s feedback submission to the Federal Reserve’s Climate Scenario Analysis draft/pilot request





Economics Tutor · March 2022 – May 2023

Provided student support for class assignments, coding, and economics course material
Led revision of homework for macroeconomics, microeconomics, and econometrics





Analytics Intern · June 2022 – August 2022

Researched macroeconomic, banking and financial sector, and industry data in Southeast Asia
Performed balance sheet and risk analysis on client firms and their competitors
Aided in data collection and visualization for industry and country reports





Student Fellow · June 2021 – August 2021

Researched and analyzed quantitative unemployment data on the low-income City of Newburgh, New York
Designed and drafted a job guarantee proposal for the City of Newburgh, NY to accompany President Biden’s Civilian Climate Corps proposal





Summer Intern · June 2020 – August 2020

Researched and presented qualitative and quantitative demographic, financial, and economic data for the City of Chicago on poverty, COVID-19, minority-owned business, and ethics reform
Responded to constituent emails and inquiries about local and state policy

title: Eli Cheam Shapiro
text:
Eli Cheam Shapiro
    Analyst, Penn Wharton Budget Model
    MRes in Economics · Sciences Po Paris

    
      I am an economist and data analyst at the
      Penn Wharton Budget Model,
      where I work on fiscal policy analysis and economic modeling.
      My work sits at the intersection of empirical economics, public finance, and data science.
    

    
      Prior to PWBM, I worked as a Predoctoral Research Assistant at Trinity College Dublin,
      where I built OCR and webscraping pipelines to digitize large-scale historical economic datasets
      from the Russian Empire and British Parliament. I hold a Master of Research in Economics from
      Sciences Po Paris (GPA: 4.0) and a BA in Economics from Bard College (GPA: 3.8).
    

    
      My research interests span macroeconomics, public finance, economic history, and
      computational methods. I am proficient in R, Python, STATA, and Julia.
